codex_response:
Implemented `fc754716` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: take the lone nonzero input cell’s color, keep the grid size unchanged, and draw a hollow border on the outer perimeter with zero interior. The Opus hint was used, and the Sonnet entry for this puzzle was also consistent with the official examples, so no `notes.md` was needed. The generator stays on-family by producing odd-sized, noise-free zero grids with a single centered colored pixel; no new primitive was necessary.

Official verification passed `5/5`, and the shared builder produced `1000` verified examples plus the expected preview artifacts. I also sanity-checked the rendered preview sheet in-terminal and sampled generated examples; they match the intended family.
